answer: Basque is an agglutinative SOV language. Key features identified: (1) Possessives use 'nire' (my) or genitive suffix '-aren/-en'; (2) Case markers: '-ekin' (with), '-n' (in), '-ra' (to); (3) Auxiliary verbs agree with subject: 'da' (3rd sg), 'dut' (I), 'du' (he/she), 'duzu' (you), 'gara' (we); (4) Negation with 'ez' before verb; (5) Adjectives follow nouns; (6) Definite article '-a' suffixed to nouns. These patterns allow systematic translation between Basque and English.
